personal.name: Himanshu
personal.subtitle: Building autonomous robots, low-level firmware systems, and lightweight Linux utilities — from the ground up.
personal.aboutSubheading: Building the future, one project at a time
bio: I'm a 10th-grade student from India with a deep passion for technology and problem-solving. My interests revolve around electronics, robotics, Arduino, and embedded systems — I love bringing ideas to life through hands-on projects.

From RC battle bots to smart farm automation systems, I'm always tinkering with something new. Math and CS are my favourite subjects, and I dream of working at NVIDIA to contribute to cutting-edge innovation.
personal.philosophy:
Engineering is learned by building.
Every failed prototype is a debugging session.
Every successful build is proof of concept.

failureLogs.resolution: 3D-printed sensor shroud to block ambient IR

failureLogs.cause: Kd too low — derivative damping insufficient
failureLogs.resolution: Increased Kd, reduced base speed threshold

failureLogs.issue: Sensor array misread at track joins
failureLogs.cause: All-black region returns no weighted center
failureLogs.resolution: Added "all-sensors-on" handling to hold last known error

failureLogs.cause: L298N driven at continuous max current
failureLogs.resolution: Added thermal heatsink; implemented current-limiting PWM ceiling

projects.pidSummary:
Error = (WeightedSensorAverage) − (CenterValue)

ControlSignal = (Kp × Error) + (Ki × IntegralOfError) + (Kd × DerivativeOfError)

Left Motor Speed  = BaseSpeed + ControlSignal
Right Motor Speed = BaseSpeed − ControlSignal

Tuned Parameters: Kp = 3.14, Ki = 0.02, Kd = 1.1

projects.title: Linux ASCII Media Engine
projects.summary: Real-time video/image to ASCII art renderer for any Linux terminal. Python-based with OpenCV, 256-color/TrueColor support.

systemParams.value: Render video/images as real-time ASCII art streams inside any Linux terminal
Python 3
OpenCV, NumPy, ANSI escape sequences
Any Linux terminal with 256-color or TrueColor support

firmwareSnippet.code:
import cv2
import numpy as np

ASCII_CHARS = " .:-=+*#%@"

def frame_to_ascii(frame, cols=80, rows=40):
    gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
    resized = cv2.resize(gray, (cols, rows * 2))
    normalized = resized / 255.0
    lines = []
    for row in resized:
        line = ""
        for pixel in row:
            idx = int((pixel / 255) * (len(ASCII_CHARS) - 1))
            line += ASCII_CHARS[idx]
        lines.append(line)
    return "\n".join(lines)

secret.title: For Soumya 💜
secret.subtitle: some things are worth keeping secret
lines: this page is for you, babita 💜
for the girl who makes my heart skip a beat every single time.
for the smile that lights up even my darkest days.
for the laugh that sounds like music to me.
for the way you roll your eyes when i call you babita.
for every little thing about you that i can't stop thinking about.
you're the first thought in my morning and the last in my night.
i love the way your eyes crinkle when you smile.
i love the way you pretend to be annoyed but laugh anyway.
i love that you're goofy, smart, and absolutely impossible to forget.
i don't know what the future holds,
but i know i want you in it.
every moment with you feels like a memory i want to keep forever.

a young South Asian girl standing in a sun-drenched courtyard, her medium-light brown skin glowing with warmth as dappled sunlight filters through the jasmine vines above. Her long, straight black hair spills like liquid silk down her back, barely stirring in the gentle breeze, while traditional gold and black jhumka earrings swing delicately from her ears, matching the stack of oxidized silver and lacquer bangles that chime softly at her wrists.
